title: thymelaea pubescens var. virgata
intro: Thymelaea pubescens var. virgata, also known as the tree daphne, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of tree daphne include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiviral properties, which make it effective against the common cold and flu. The plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in the form of essential oil, which is extracted through steam distillation, to harness its medicinal properties.
